apply: Apply the universal law of gravitation to calculate the force of attraction between two objects
Use the concept of gravitational potential energy to solve problems related to the motion of objects under the influence of gravity
Calculate the escape velocity from the surface of the Earth and other celestial bodies
Apply the concept of gravitational field to determine the acceleration due to gravity at any point on or near the surface of the Earth
analyze: Analyze the factors affecting the value of g and calculate its variation with latitude and altitude
Compare the gravitational potential energy of an object at different heights and depths
Analyze the motion of objects under the influence of gravity in a rotating frame of reference
Compare the gravitational fields of the Earth and other celestial bodies
remember: Recall the law of gravitation and its limitations
State the universal law of gravitation and its mathematical formulation
List the factors affecting the acceleration due to gravity
Recall the concept of free fall and the conditions for it to occur
understand: Understand the concept of gravitational potential energy and its relationship with the height of an object
Explain the concept of escape velocity and its dependence on the mass of the object and the distance from the center of the Earth
Describe the concept of gravitational potential and its variation with distance from the center of the Earth
Understand the concept of gravitational field and its application to the motion of objects near the surface of the Earth

ncert_objectives: State the universal law of gravitation and derive its mathematical formulation.
Define the acceleration due to gravity and distinguish between its value on the surface of the Earth and at heights above the ground.
Explain how the value of g varies with latitude and altitude.
Derive an expression for the gravitational potential energy of an object at a height h above the ground in terms of the universal constant G, the mass of the Earth M, and the height h.
board_exam_objectives: Define universal law of gravitation and derive its mathematical formulation.
Describe the factors affecting the acceleration due to gravity.
Derive an expression for the gravitational potential energy of an object at a height h above the ground.
Explain the concept of escape velocity.
